Label37.name: Label37
Label37.label: Enter any information that may be useful during the planning stage of the audit.

Label40.name: Label40
Label40.label: In this tab, you can select which capabilities, if any, need to be audited.

Opportunities.name: Opportunities
Opportunities.label: Opportunities
Opportunities.tooltip:
Opportunities for improving the audit program can include:
• Allowing multiple audits to be conducted in a single visit.
• Minimizing time and distance involved in travelling between audit sites.
• Matching the level of competence of the audit team to the level of competence needed to achieve the audit objectives.
• Aligning audit dates with the availability of auditees’ key staff.

Label27.name: Label27
Label27.label: Please enter some comments / notes about this audit

Team.name: Team
Team.label: Team
Team.tooltip: Select the team that will conduct this audit.

Leader.name: Leader
Leader.label: Team Leader
Leader.tooltip: The team leader is based on the selected team.

Label1.name: Label1
Label1.label: Link this audit definition to  Processes, Services or Products using the fields below.

FinancialRessourceGrid.name: FinancialRessourceGrid
FinancialRessourceGrid.label: Financial Resources
FinancialRessourceGrid.tooltip: What are the financial and temporal resources necessary to develop, implement, manage, and improve audit activities?

Label2.name: Label2
Label2.label: These requirements will be assessed during the audit.

Risk.name: Risk
Risk.label: Risks
Risk.tooltip:
Risks associated with an audit program that can affect the achievement of its objectives. Risks must be identified and presented to auditors so they can be considered and addressed appropriately. These risks can relate to:
• Planning (e.g. failure to set relevant audit objectives and define the extent, scope, duration, locations, and schedule of the audit).
• Resources (e.g. insufficient allocation of time, equipment, and/or training for the development of the audit program or conducting the audit).
• Selection of the audit team (e.g. insufficient overall competence or experience to conduct audits effectively).
• Communication (e.g. ineffective external/internal communication processes or channels).
• Implementation (e.g. ineffective coordination of the audits within the audit program or disregarding of information security and confidentiality).
• Control of documented information (e.g. ineffective determination of the documented information required by auditors and relevant interested parties, or failure to adequately protect audit records to demonstrate audit program efficacy).
• Monitoring, reviewing, and improving the audit program (e.g. ineffective monitoring of audit program outcomes).
• Availability and cooperation of auditee(s) and availability of evidence to be sampled.

Criteria.name: Criteria
Criteria.label: Criteria
Criteria.tooltip:
Audit criteria are used as a reference against which conformity is determined. These standards may include:
• Applicable policies, processes, procedures, performance criteria (including objectives, statutory and regulatory requirements, and management system requirements).
• Information regarding the context and the risks and opportunities as determined by the auditee (including relevant external/internal interested parties’ requirements).
• Sector codes of conduct or other planned arrangements.

Scope.name: Scope
Scope.label: Scope
Scope.tooltip: The audit scope should be consistent with the audit program and objectives. It includes such factors as locations, functions, activities, and processes to be audited, as well as the time period covered by the audit.

Objectives.name: Objectives
Objectives.label: Objectives
Objectives.tooltip:
Examples of audit objectives include:
• Identify opportunities for the improvement of a management system and its performance.
• Evaluate the effectiveness of management systems and associated controls.
• Identify opportunities for the improvement of a management system and its performance.
• Validate the protection of organizational assets from loss or unauthorized use while evaluating the effectiveness of existing controls.

Availabilityofinformation.name: Availabilityofinformation
Availabilityofinformation.label: The availability of information and communication technologies (e.g., technical resources required to set up a remote audit using technologies that support remote collaboration)

Availabilityofanytoolstechnologyandequip.name: Availabilityofanytoolstechnologyandequipmentrequired
Availabilityofanytoolstechnologyandequip.label: The availability of any required tools, technology, and equipment

Requirementsrelatedtothefacility.name: Requirementsrelatedtothefacility
Requirementsrelatedtothefacility.label: Requirements related to the facility, including any security clearances and equipment (e.g., background checks, personal protective equipment or clean room attire)

AuditReportingOutput.name: AuditReportingOutput
AuditReportingOutput.label: Required audit reporting output, to whom said output is to be distributed, and any matters relating to confidentiality and information security

Anyhealthsafety.name: Anyhealthsafety
Anyhealthsafety.label: Any health, safety, and environmental arrangements for the auditors

Requirementsfortravel.name: Requirementsfortravel
Requirementsfortravel.label: Requirements for travel or access to remote sites

ReasonforAudit.label: Describe the reason for this audit (optional)

Department.name: Department

Department.tooltip: Select the parent department relevant to the audit. Your choice will affect the available options in the Business Line dropdown.

BusinessLine.name: BusinessLine
BusinessLine.label: Business Line
BusinessLine.tooltip: Select the child department ofthe Business Division relevant to the audit.

PotentialAuditors.name: PotentialAuditors
PotentialAuditors.label: Participant Auditors
PotentialAuditors.tooltip: Potential auditors will include all members of the selected team by default, but can be further defined as needed.

Estimatedeffort.tooltip: Specify the estimated number of hours it will take to execute the audit.

Daysbeforescheduleddateforauditexecution.tooltip: Specify how many days before the scheduled audit start date to send the first reminder to participants.

QMS_LanguageTitle.name: QMS_LanguageTitle
QMS_LanguageTitle.label: Content Language
QMS_LanguageTitle.tooltip: Specify the language to be used when entering data into forms.
